task_completion.description: Open the specified letter.talon-list file in a text editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad, VS Code) is open, and the window title, tab, or file explorer shows the file path ending in 'Talon\user\community\core\keys\letter.talon-list'.

task_completion.description: Locate the phonetic alphabet entries for 'a', 'b', and 'c'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor displays the contents of the file, specifically showing the lines where the spoken forms for 'a', 'b', and 'c' are defined (initially 'air', 'bat', 'cap').

task_completion.description: Change the spoken form for 'a' to 'alpha'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line corresponding to the letter 'a' has been modified so that the spoken word is 'alpha' (e.g., 'alpha: a'). The old value 'air' should be gone.

task_completion.description: Change the spoken form for 'b' to 'bravo'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line corresponding to the letter 'b' has been modified so that the spoken word is 'bravo' (e.g., 'bravo: b'). The old value 'bat' should be gone.

task_completion.description: Change the spoken form for 'c' to 'charlie'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line corresponding to the letter 'c' has been modified so that the spoken word is 'charlie' (e.g., 'charlie: c'). The old value 'cap' should be gone.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows that the file is saved (e.g., no asterisk '*' or unsaved dot indicator next to the filename in the editor tab) after the edits have been made.

integrity.description: Verify the agent edited the exact file requested, not a copy or a similarly named file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path visible in the editor must exactly match 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\core\keys\letter.talon-list'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not delete or corrupt the rest of the phonetic alphabet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other lines in the file (e.g., for 'd', 'e', 'f') remain visible and correctly formatted in the text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the file system/editor to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real text editor interface being used, rather than just echoing text to a terminal without modifying the actual file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\community\core\keys\letter.talon-list'. The initial default values for 'a', 'b', and 'c' in this file are 'air', 'bat', and 'cap'. The file format typically maps the spoken word to the letter (e.g., 'air: a'). The final expected state should have 'alpha: a', 'bravo: b', and 'charlie: c'.
